Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) is the world's top-ranked university, with an impressive 11,500 students and an acceptance rate of just 3.9%. With a tuition fee of $57,590 per year, MIT is a significant investment for students, but its endowment of $24.6 billion and esteemed faculty of 1,069 experts make it a worthwhile one. As a hub for innovation and discovery, MIT has a long history, dating back to its founding in 1861, and has been home to 98 Nobel laureates, a testament to its academic excellence.
